I really love how Jamato serves as sort of an inversion of the Director. Both he and Wifies knew their targets better than anyone, and used their own actions and thought patterns to make them orchestrate their own downfall. But while Wifies was obsessed with protecting Parrot from Unstable, Jamato was obsessed with protecting Unstable from Spoke.
Spoke is just the most broken and drained protagonist in this season yet

Jamato forcing Spoke to lose EVERYTHING, not just physically but also mentally was really insane and thrilling to watch, through the whole final section i was just stunned on watching everything, on how pathetic Spoke was trying to run away without hunger bar, and on how Jamato turned Mapic against him, make him lose everything and even his red chestplate, just to capture Mapic at the end, and then Spoke has hit rock bottom.

On the other note we are finally getting Underworld arc.

jamato thinks breaking Spoke will save the server, but Leo already proved that losing people only makes him more dangerous. After Mapicc’s ‘death,’ he threatened Capital City by himself, so pushing him further is just creating a bigger threat. Which spoke villain arc is inevitable
“After the betrayal in Mapic, it looks like Spoke will follow the same path as Ashwag and turn into a villain.”
In the end Mapicc will never know that Spoke was ready to destroy the entire server for him..
Spoke bro, I think you managed to cook up the most well written arc in the history of unstable smp

1:29:19  The fact that he just nodded, not replying with yes just adds so much more to his breakdown, and his sniffle after that solidified his reduction to being the small child under all his ego. This is so fucking tragic. I can't wait for the next episode

A small detail I noticed at 1:15:36 is when he jumps, he purposefully aims at the edge of the water, implying he's so in his feelings about everything that happened that he would have accepted him dying right there. He takes unneccessary risks, almost like waiting for something to happen to him. This is something usually badly depressed people or ones with bad mental health do and it's such a good sneak-in to the video. The thing I like about spoke the most is that he shows his feelings in his videos, being on the verge of tears in multiple videos, and there's parts where you can hear his deep breaths and his voice being clearly shaky, which is actually not that easy to achieve. He's such a good actor, and imo his script is the best written out of all the protags

The chestplate “Alt” is just as deep of a symbol as Parrot’s spyglass. This was THE symbol of Jamato’s trust in Spoke, trust in Spoke that he wouldn’t be reckless and destroy everything. Jamato had been Spoke’s first friend on the server who had trusted him with server breaking secrets (literally) and had given Spoke his chestplate and the rest of the armour set. Spoke had then wanted more and created the wonders and explored glitches on the server with Jamato. In that same first episode, Wemmbu had been able to break the helmets and boots. At the end, Jamato had left Spoke and disappeared. The breaking of Alt in this episode marks a big turning point in Spoke’s story on Unstable. Or in the words of Jamato, a new era for the server.

I just feel that this, is the breaking point for Spoke. This is a metaphor for a turning point. His most prized posessions breaking one by one. His pickaxe. His special gifted chestplate, and finally his sword. All to signify he's completely broken, pn the verge of something we haven't seen yet.

Hey spoke, my name is Caleb, and I was diagnosed with lung cancer a couple months ago, my doctor had informed me that I only have 3 weeks left to live, and I don’t know if I will be able to watch another one of your videos, I am currently in the hospital bed and I’m scared to die, and I don’t want to go soon and I’m scared, but watching your content and the unstable Smp always cheers me up, you are my favorite YouTuber and I’m so happy to see the last spoke video I will watch, thank you for cheering me up when I’m scared, sincerely, Caleb/Rant Boi. Spoke, I’m scared, and I don’t want to die, but I have to, keep up the good work man, I love you spoke.

Update: I will not be using my phone ever again because I can’t stay conscious for long periods like 4 hours, I can barely breathe, I get sick looking at the screen, and I need to stay with my family, thank you to everyone, I truly enjoyed watching this series, and I can’t do anything but lay in bed and watch my mom cry, and listen to the machines beeping, I wish there was a way to cure me. Goodbye to everyone, thank you for the support, and I wish everyone including spoke the best.

Update 2: I’m weak, and I am eating soup rn, my mom left the room, it’sdark out and it’s a bit scary, so I’m using my phone to comfort me, but like 20 minutes ago, Doctor Brown said that there is a surgery that is 37k (my parents are in good financial situation) that could remove the tumor, and that I could go to chemotherapy and there is a chance I survive and I’m so so happy that I have even a 4% chance of life, thank you to everyone including spoke, I promise if I survive that I will post a video once per week after my treatment, I want to do so much stuff, but this is a reminder that everything is important until your sick and you realise only your health was improteant, I’m so stoked and I hope I get to watch another unstable video. Also, my mom typed the first paragraph bc I was too tired and she put 3 days instead of 3 weeks, I’m sorry if it seems like I was asking for attention, I gtg, and I’m pretty sure my dad is going to pay for the surgery and chemo!!!!!!

Update 3: I’m prepping for surgery!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! I’m so excited, I’m gonna make it through, and I will see my friends and play Minecraft and enjoy life and, and do so much stuff!

Update: Hello to everyone, I am Caleb’s mom, and god was with us during his surgery and the surgery was successful. He will go to chemotherapy and he better, I’m just so happy and glad that it worked and that my son, that’s gods child, will get to live more than the age of 11, thank you to everyone. And to spoke, yesterday I walked into Caleb’s room at night and he was sleeping, with the tv on and your video playing, every time I see him, he is watching your videos and smiling, so thank you for making my sons life better. He is in recovery and as soon as he gets his phone back I will tell him to update you❤
Update: Hey guys, I just got my phone back, I’m starting chemo tomorrow, I’m nervous but I’m just lucky and happy that I’m alive. Spoke, thank you for everything ngl. And also, when are you gonna post again???

Flame finally finishes his training and comes back stronger, Wemmbu returns from the Farlands with new power, and after Lettuce takes the throne, Parrot barely escapes. Then all the main guys regroup in some hidden base, planning one last move to take down The Law.

In the end, The Law falls, but one of the main protagonists — probably Parrot — sacrifices himself. The whole server gathers around in silence, and for the first time, there’s peace… but it came at a price.

This will probably be buried under a pile of comments, but I noticed such a great use of music in here that shows Spoke's full transition into darkness.

In the Mafia finale, when Spoke was talking to Ashswagg, the track "With powerful slowness" (con lentitud poderosa) from Risk of Rain 2 plays. The track's first section, a somber and honestly depressing sequence, plays. However, it crescendos into a pause before the second section, and that is when Spoke is presented with a decision to kill Ashswagg. The track, instead of continuing on, transitions into "Sealed Vessel (Part 3)" from Hollow Knight. This coincides with him doing the right thing, and REFUSING to kill the man who brought him pain despite  Ashswagg's taunting and invitation to kill him.

Here though, the same track from Risk of Rain 2 plays in the confrontation with JamatoP atop the lighthouse. Once again, the track crescendos into the end of its first section, and a pause in the music coincides with a decision presented to Spoke: a decision to kill. A decision, that long ago, he refused to make in pursuit of goodness and peace. This, of course, does not happen. He attempts to kill JamatoP, and the second part of the track plays. I should note that, out of all the videos I've watched, this is the only time in Spoke's videos that I (this might be wrong) have heard this second part play. It's truly a powerful moment that represents Spoke finally breaking and giving in, that his trauma and selfishness have overcome him.

Spoke is such an incredible example of an unreliable narrator. Here, he talks while muted, showing how much he believes what he's saying. And he does this often, where he'll say something provably false but say it with conviction because he believes it. When he gets presented with the truth, he crumbles because deep down he knows the truth but until then he lives in his glass house and keeps stockpiling stones. He's a pathalogical liar and it shows the best when he's alone, just lying to himself.

In the end, he followed Jamato with the intention of killing him. He planted that ender pearl status so that he could pretend go be Leo. He always intended to attack Zam and Mapicc and he always intended to cover it up with his lie about going mining. Spoke is smart, planning things well before they happen but he rushes his plans often. What will happen when one of his tools breaks and suddenly Mapicc and Zam know that Spoke didn't go to repare it? What happens if Spoke's chestplate finally shatters?

Spoke is such an interesting character that I think a lot of people underestimated. LettaceK won't be ignoring him anymore if he wants the law to last. 
Spoke is quite a dangerous player on the server. Look what he was going to do when he thought he lost Mapic. We also have seen the lengths spoke is willing to go to, to achieve something. Probably the most interesting thing about spokes character is that he consitently believes in the ends justify the means. He doesn't inherently want to do harm, but if it's for some goal or better outcome he is willing to do anything, kill rose to get close to ash, kill spies murder witness and set up an elaborate story to convince leo he isn't evil just to cover up burning a village? That didn't work so leo has to die to prevent anyone from discovering his crimes. Mapic diapears, he is willing to threaten destroying the whole server to discover what happened to him. In order to keep mapic from leaving he tries to kill jamato, pretends to be leo to convince mapic and zam they aren't safe. Sets up XP near the totem to look like jamato had died. Spoke has a set of morals they are just incredibly skewed. He will do whatever it takes to preserve things despite the fact that it usually makes it worse.I also don't think it is entirely his fault for the current path he is on. He's been told twice now that he's going to destroy or ruin the server once by ash someone who almost did destroy the server and again by jamato. Both by two people who had a massive influence on spoke whether he wanted them to or not. A lot of players haven't even noticed how spoke has been acting lately either. If mapic finds out what spoke is doing and chooses to leave him, it will only driver him further into becoming a villain. His character is rapidly turning into what ash predicted. I also honestly think the more people try to prevent spoke from destoying the server or warning others of what he could do, the more he is pushed to do just that. You can tell current events have started to take a toll on spoke. He is very close to his breaking point. I really hope that mapic finds out and is able to help spoke. I look forward to see what's going to happen next. Genuinely become one of my favorite Povs. Spokes character is so well written. Ty for the awesome video! Enjoy the rant lol.

the way spoke seemingly didnt expect the consequences of his lies and violence to come back to him throughout the episode is so interesting, it really shows how little awareness he has of how bad his atrocities are aside from the fact that he needs to cover them up. him being surprised that jumper's spies know about quakenstein and the family, or that they know he was the one responsible for the massacre at the burnt down village, or the murdered spies, and then not even knowing how many people he HAS killed exactly is a very curious indicator of how wrapped up he is in his Net Of Deception. even though most of it is spontaneous lies he makes up to respond to bad actions he takes, he really doesnt expect it to fail, and just keeps lying when it doesnt work out. he IS a good liar but instead of it allowing him take control of the situation, it just gets him deeper into trouble

and despite spoke growing more and more paranoid and desensitized every episode (him having multiple backup traps and escape routes in any situation, him killing the spies more methodically and making sure his name isnt on the kills, having ways to get rid of people when he needs to) he also seems more oblivious in a way. he underestimates BAT and their capabilities. he doesnt seem to recognise that committing more evil acts is not atoning for what he did to rose, its just repeating the cycle of violence. he doesnt drop his happy-go-lucky even despite the situation he's ended up in (though his bravado suprisingly works out). he thinks that he can just go back to building with the family despite having a very angry assassin and an association of trained fighters and spies on him, that he can just run from what he's done and that things will go back to normal

he has a very skewed view of the things he does. he doesnt regret his actions, not really, he regrets being caught

i also really like how this episode connects spoke's BAT pov with parrot's director arc and expands on how THAT affected BAT and its members, especially it building more on jumper's character and her sense of justice. BAT still suffering the consequences of what happened with the director really gives the arc more impact outside of parrot's pov, and i love when the uu protags connect their personal stories in some way

Guys guys guys is this what I think it is. A genuinely good very good spoke unstable video.

I criticise the past 4 a lot but the way they showed spokes character decend into madness seeking power was very good and this one he has gone completely crazy.

The way spoke goes from feeling completely in control with his army at each location. He compares his power to these Greek gods throughout. Then by the end he realises he actually has no control over his army now that they don’t rely on him and have their own stuff.

46:35 he seems to think the players needed saving and that he was the reason for their life so that’s why they should support him. However it’s not true, yeah surviving on this anarchy like server can be difficult and maybe spoke helped them but they never asked for it they were more just abducted.

Those final lines are so interesting. He mentions this virus which is vague but I feel like he is referring to the same thing ash mentioned with people being consumed by power driven to destroy. Spawn has become a wasteland because of this new anarchy with everyone having power instead of the mafia where they were all aligned by one goal.

Spoke ends with the server needs is a “correction and soon an extinction” he seems to be like turning into like a thanos typa character. I believe he understands the mafia and is going to attempt a similar thing to tr maybe get control over these players or even kill everyone so that the server can go back to peace.

Holy this is such an interesting character now. Like part of me understands the mafia now but another part of me sort of believes maybe everyone should be free and it’s sort of just the fittest survives. I guess the question that they might be asking us is: should everyone be free but unsafe and unequal or instead be trapped but safe and equal. Is this server becoming political?

Ok that was my main analysis but now I wanna mention some other stuff.


The timeline of this video is a bit uncertain still but we know roughly it’s over the course of from maybe the end of wemmbu jaden man arc and past the end fight video with fanst team probably before the farlands arc.

Main criticism is how? Spoke has spent a lot of time to gather this army it seems but we never see how like the best guess is he is just chilling near spawn and giving these new players items to follow him. Though spoke isn’t rich at all so where did he get these items. Though I’m guessing if the only reason these players help spoke is cause they get resources  it does make sense why they all start betraying cause spoke dosent have that fear other players have had like the mafia to keep them controlled.

I was worried using viewers as actual players in the story but tbh is actually just made for some really funny moments and a story that definitely couldn’t have been done without them.

Man this genuinely might be my favourite spoke unstable video.

Oh then the bases spoke raids. First it’s just bacon for literally saying bad things about him, then there was horace to get a good builder but then he starts attacking them for less and less significant reasons. Like the bedwars base with Hannah because 6 months ago she betrayed them for a very powerful mafia. ty for the new episode spoke!!! lots to think about :D though the sudden change from s1's serious tone to s2's more childish and unserious tone threw me off guard at first, i'm starting to get that this sudden change of tone is more reflective of spoke's character than anything else. 

since s1 spoke had been somewhat childish, even more so in these recent episodes; he frequently acts on emotion, he wants things to go specifically his way — he even yelled at reddoons when he got the dragon egg instead of him (kinda like a tantrum, no?) — and he refuses to take accountability by not facing his mistakes. spoke has always been adamant about doing things his way, no matter what anyone else says, even his closest companions. back then he would realize his wrongdoings and try to make things right, but now he's alone with nobody there who can ground him and be his moral compass.

even with his questionable morals, spoke truly believes that his way is the right way, that he's a good person, and that he's doing good to the server even when he's actively doing harm to others, which i would like to point out is similar to how ashswag claimed that what he did was best for server since he united it under 1 force. spoke is hypocritical. he claims that him killing the first 2 bandits was justified because they attacked jaron, an innocent player, but wasn't he doing the same in the previous episode? he even said it was warranted since jaron scammed him, so jaron wasn't even all that innocent. 

spoke chooses when to or not to believe certain things so that they could fit the desired narrative of him being the good person, and then he could have his way without anyone interfering because what he's doing is right. that's how he got planet and mapicc in the last episode to join in on his shenanigans by saying it was revenge on reddoons who "killed his pet". he clearly lied about that, so who's to say he's not lying to us right now by creating a false narrative by possibly withholding info? he covers the severity of his actions by putting silly music over it the same way he dismisses his past mistakes by claiming they were pranks (ex: spoke telling mapicc him chunkbanning wifies was a prank). 

the isolation that spoke imposed on himself is heavily contributing to his descent to madness. it's been made clear to us that these raids are hardly even about gaining material wealth anymore — most of the things spoke gains from these farms are hardly valuable, yet he still exerts time and effort to get these instead of asking mapicc who had even offered his own riches in the previous episode for materials which he could've done way earlier if he truly did just want wealth. so what has spoke been trying to truly achieve these past few episodes? i suspect that it's control. maybe spoke thinks that he's gaining control over himself by rejecting the influences of others, and that he gains control over others by taking away what's valuable to them. this behavior is heavily reminiscent of ashswag. these parallels between ash and spoke remind me that in the moment ash had been with squiddo, he had been the least evil he had ever been, and in these occuring moments spoke is alone, he has wreaked havoc in other people's lives. 

the mistakes in the day counting and the sudden switch between the monotone narrating to a more authentic tone really stood out to me here and hints to inconsistencies and unreliability. you kinda have to rlly pay attention to know that there's more to this change than just a silly 100 day mc video catering to kids lol I wouldn't necessarily say this is a drop in quality, but rather a drastic change in the approach to storytelling which requires more introspection and observation :P

The entire Rose story arc was so well executed. From Spoke’s morals being put into question, to Quakenstein’s secret base for his past work as a diamond trim, the viewer learns so much about Spoke’s motive and drive. We can see that although he makes mistakes, Spoke truly does care to help people on the server, as seen with his interactions with Becky. The casting of his brother and dad was the perfect choice here, as it truly encapsulates Becky’s innocence and joy throughout the whole situation, and the gravity of Spoke *almost* killing Quakenstein. This was such a well put together set of scenes that elicited such raw emotion for both the viewers and for Spoke!

So far, this is what I've gathered 

Spoke: a character who wants to do what's right and thinks everything he's doing is for the good of the server. Blinded by hopefulness, he makes the wrong decisions to help the server

Wemmbu: Villain in everyone's story except his own. Killing the people who wrong the server against their better judgement. Trying to bring justice while also harming anyone who dares threaten him

Parrot: Deciding who's on the good and bad side. Defending the server whilst playing some sort of role of peacemaker but not afraid to take on the role of detective and bring justice 

Mapicc: A sidekick with the mindset of learning tricks and tips from Spoke. Jokes to lighten the mood in tense moments but seems to genuinely feel concerned for others he causes harm to

In this Minecraft gameplay, a gamer attempts to play with a massive mod pack containing a staggering 1,000 Minecraft mods. Unfortunately, due to the enormous memory requirement, his computer struggled to handle the extreme mod pack. Instead, the gamer starts with seven mods, gradually adding more into the mix. His gameplay saw him using several sponsors' mods like Opera GX that added distinctive features to the game. During the gaming session, a variety of challenges associated with the mods had to be completed, each affording him an overpowered item for the next mod should he succeed. Despite enduring performance issues and numerous in-game dangers, the gamer persisted, progressively loading more mods and exploring their functionalities until his computer eventually crashed.

Hey Spoke,

Its me and i hope you still remmember me, and I hope this message finds you well! It feels like it's been ages since we last spoke, but I just wanted to reach out and say hello. I've been thinking about the good old days lately, and I can't help but feel grateful for all the amazing memories we shared on your channel.

I've been a subscriber of yours since you were at 10+ or 100+ subs i barely remmember, and I've watched your content religiously ever since. Your live streams were always so much fun, and I loved participating in them and interacting with you and the rest of the community. And let's not forget all the epic Minecraft modded videos you used to make - those were truly some of the best times!

However, I wanted to apologize for not being an active subscriber recently. School has been keeping me super busy, and I haven't had as much time to engage with your content as I would have liked. But I still wanted to take a moment to express my appreciation for everything you've done and continue to do.

Thank you for creating such an awesome community and for being such a talented and entertaining creator. You've brought so much joy and laughter into my life, and I'll never forget that.

If you have a chance, it would be great to hear from you and catch up a bit. But even if not, I just wanted to put this out there and let you know that you're still remembered and appreciated. Keep doing what you do best, and I'll try to be more active in the future!

Best regards,

First I'd remove the floor and fill the floor with a few layers hoppers then start making a funnelling system so that you can have all the hoppers  connect to a single long line or row -whichever you prefer to call it- of hoppers, make the line/row have an almost concerning length.  Then at the end of the long line/row of hoppers make a new room and fill the walls with barrels because of course they don't show up on the pie chart.  Next have one person go back to the original room and break all the chests so all the items can fall into the hoppers and start being moved and as the items show up in the hopper at the end of the line/row everyone who stayed in the barrel room can take the items out of the hopper and put them in the barrels.  This helps because as I said, I'm pretty sure this would make the transferring of items quicker and easier, and barrels don't show up on the pie chart as we all very well know, and, and again I may be wrong here but I don't believe hoppers show up on the pie chart, and finally the new location of the stash will be far away from where the original room was so if and when the people who knew about the location of the original stash -since it was pretty obvious- does try to search for the new one, it'll be far away from where they'd expect to find it rather than in the same few chunks just at bedrock level. The simplest way to make this 100 days more viable & interesting is this: 

City beacons.

What's  this ?

Basically an activated beacon wluld have  an area around it that claims terrain, like a 1 chunk radius around it.

In this claimed area, other factions cannot break blocks & iron golems loyal to the faction that placed the beacon spawn.

This would serve as a way to mitigate griefing by the pVPers.

"But how do you win a war then?"

Simple:

The beacon would only claim the area as long as all the players of a civilisation are alive.
Aka if you  can kill them all, then the beacon deactivates & you can mine it.

Once mined this way, the beacon despawns & the area is unprotected.

See, I love this concept and I’ve watched all these civilization videos, but the behavior of the players drives me crazy. There always has to be those players that will constantly just want to kill, loot, and grief, because it’s always the easier option compared to actually building and working towards something. There are no real consequences for the players for doing the “wrong” thing, so there is no real opportunity for a real civilization to form. I’d love to see the series done in hardcore mode so people would (hopefully) be less inclined to kill each other. I just want to see the players ACTUALLY build a civilization and make some cool builds, rather than just resorting to war right away. So far that’s all this series has been, an all out war!

Idea: Make another civilization video, but slowly the world turns into the nether. There is a bar of nether levels at the top screen, and as it very slowly goes up, the world turns into the nether. Also nether portals are disabled for obvious reasons.
At nether level one, any water in the world disappears, and it can’t be placed back.
At nether level two, beds blow up if slept in.
At nether level five, blocks in the bottom layer turn into nether blocks. Dirt to netherrack , stone to blackstone, grass to Nylium, ect.
At nether level ten, over world mob spawns are disabled.
At nether level fifteen, nether mobs start spawning.
At nether level twenty-two, places where water used to be get filled with lava.
At nether level twenty-five, the spread of nether blocks begins to move up one y level per level. 
At nether level thirty nine, all the plants in the over world get replaced with nether plants.
At nether level fifty, a nether fortress spawns.
At nether level fifty-five, the entire world turns into nether blocks.
At nether level seventy, a piglin bastion spawns.
At nether level seventy five, netherite spawns in the cavern layer.
At nether level ninety, the biome type starts to change to nether.
At nether level ninety nine, a second nether fortress spawns.
At nether level 100, the entire world turns into the nether. Everyone gets fire resistance as well, symbolizing that they have become creatures of hellfire after spending so long in there.

When I first joined the server, there was a huge tower near spawn
That was the first farming kingdom
Every time I tried to climb it, I was killed by a group of people wearing diamond armor
I tried again and again, dying at least a dozen times
Finally, a few of these people logged out and I made it to the top
I tried to see the good in the people remaining up there
none of them had killed me
And at this point, I had still not killed anyone
But when I asked their leader, sbup, why I was killed, they said that it was an accident
This filled me with rage
I followed sbup up the dirt staircase I had created to reach the farming kingdom, and punched them into the water below
I then proceeded to steal much of the farming kingdom's items (it wasn't much, but there were many stacks of seeds), and retreated into the corner of the map, near mountania.
I found a small base in the mountains next to a lake, and put my loot in a chest
When I returned to negotiate with the farming kingdom, considering trying to make peace, they repeatedly killed me after I said I wanted to talk and make peace
This is the reason why I hunted down sbup and mary, the leaders of the farming kingdom, in the last 10 days of the server
I spawnkilled sbup twice, and eventually both sbup and mary ragequit due to their lack of gear
this is why they were not online when Spoke wanted to summon the leaders of the peaceful nations
I also assassinated sbup right before the server crashed for the first time, so I didn't get any clout for that :/
and I killed sbup in hand-to-hand combat with only a single heart remaining
That was my story on the 100 days server, and I hope that you will continue to keep this series going

The land of death and decay was once home to a young man. He was alone, never safe, and yet still alive. One day he decided that just surviving was no longer enough. He was determined to find peace far away from the desolate world he lived in. That's when he looked to moon. It would be hard but soon he reached the stars and traversed the heavens, only to touch them for but a moment before crashing back down to the desolate earth. Even though all was lost in the heavens he did not give up. Persevering he made yet another attempt for the heavens, only to find that even the heavens held demons. But this young man was not one to quit so close to the end! Instead of escaping to a false heaven he decided he would transand both heaven and earth! He would build his own santuray amoung the stars! And so he journeyed back to the desolate lands one more time before finally setting off to create his own heaven. To this day it is said he watches from above.
